title: file-result delegates the paused_for_research outcome to spawn_research (engine-atomic), not the worker
context: The continuation engine (sb/spawn.py::spawn_research) already creates the research task, re-enqueues the parent as a continuation depending on it, carries the partial forward, cycle-checks, and caps chain depth. What was missing: how a subagent signals a research handoff (it can only speak through its result file) and who turns that into a spawn. The result outcome enum had no paused_for_research. Director confirmed the direction before planning.

options.name: engine-atomic
options.rationale: Add a paused_for_research outcome + a research block to the result schema; file-result sees that outcome and calls spawn_research itself, then returns. The worker just files as always — one validated door.
options.tradeoff: file-result gains a branch and a `from sb import spawn` import (no cycle: spawn doesn't import results). spawn_research already owns the full lane transition, so the branch must return early and skip the normal embed/move/remove.

options.name: worker-orchestrates
options.rationale: The worker reads the paused_for_research result, then calls `sb spawn --task ... --goal ... --tier ... --done ...` itself, then continues.
options.tradeoff: Keeps file-result unchanged, but splits one logical transition across two CLI calls in the prose skill (un-atomic): a crash between file and spawn leaves the parent active with a stale result file, recovered only by lease expiry. Also duplicates the research-request plumbing in the prompt.

reasoning: file-result is already 'the only door results enter through'; making the research handoff one more outcome it routes keeps that invariant and makes the whole transition atomic and validated in one place, reusing the tested spawn_research. The worker stays dumb (file as normal). The alternative spreads a single state transition across two prose-driven CLI calls, reintroducing exactly the crash-window/duplicate-plumbing the single-door design exists to avoid.

steelman.strongest_case: It keeps the engine's file-result smaller and avoids coupling results.py to spawn.py; `sb spawn` already exists as a public verb meant to be called, so using it from the worker is the 'intended' seam, and the crash window is already an accepted risk documented in spawn.py.

blast_radius: One branch in file_result + a `from sb import spawn` import; a result-schema bump (0.1.0->0.2.0) touching every result writer. No change to spawn_research itself. Reversible: the branch could be removed and the worker taught to call `sb spawn` instead, with no data migration.

feedback.note: Ratified by merging PR #3 (engine-atomic file-result delegation to spawn_research). Direction was confirmed before planning; merge is the binding gate (PHI-028).
